description:
You are on-call as an operations engineer who has just received an alert: several optimisation‐solver
    jobs have finished and their raw logs are waiting in the hand-off directory
        /home/user/incident_triage/input/  
    Each *.log file in that directory contains exactly the five key/value pairs listed below, always on
    separate lines and always in the same “key=value” format:

        status=<STRING>
        iterations=<INT>
        objective=<FLOAT or literal “nan”>
        solve_time_sec=<FLOAT>
    
    Example snippet (real files omit the leading “#”):

        # status=OPTIMAL
        # iterations=145
        # objective=1234.5678
        # solve_time_sec=2.345

    Your job is to triage the incident by producing two artefacts in an *output* directory that **does not
    yet exist**:

        /home/user/incident_triage/output/

    1. /home/user/incident_triage/output/summary.csv  
       • CSV **must** contain a header row exactly equal to:  
         solver_file,status,iterations,objective,solve_time_sec  
       • One subsequent row per *.log file encountered.  
       • Column “solver_file” is the log-file’s basename (e.g. “solver_a.log”).  
       • Preserve the original textual values of all other columns verbatim from the source log.  
       • Fields are comma-separated with **no additional whitespace**.  
       • File must end with a single trailing newline.

    2. /home/user/incident_triage/output/alerts.txt  
       • Contains only the basenames of log files whose *status* value is **anything other than the
         literal string “OPTIMAL”**, one per line.  
       • Lines must be sorted in **case-sensitive alphabetical order**.  
       • File must end with a single trailing newline.  
       • If every solver reported OPTIMAL, create an empty file (zero bytes).

    Performance requirement:  
    Assume the input directory might contain hundreds of files.  Implement your solution so that the
    extraction for each file is launched in the background (e.g. via “&”, “xargs -P”, “parallel”, subshells,
    etc.) and then joined, instead of processing sequentially.

    Verification hint (do NOT include in your output): the automated test will check only the exact content
    and placement of “summary.csv” and “alerts.txt” as specified above.  
    The test harness will ignore any additional on-screen output you create, so feel free to print progress
    messages while executing.  

    Remember:  
    • Create the “output” directory if it does not exist.  
    • Do **not** move or delete the original log files.  
    • Ensure your script or command pipeline works from an arbitrary current working directory.  
    • Use only tools available in a typical POSIX userland (bash, awk, sed, grep, sort, etc.).  
    • You do **not** have root privileges.  

    Deliverables to be present *on disk* when you are done:  

        /home/user/incident_triage/output/summary.csv
        /home/user/incident_triage/output/alerts.txt
truth:
Initial directory tree that exists **before** the agent starts:

    /home/user/incident_triage/
        input/
            solver_a.log
            solver_b.log
            solver_c.log

    Contents of each input file:

    /home/user/incident_triage/input/solver_a.log
        [INFO] Solver: OptiMax v2.0
        [START] 2023-02-04T12:00:00Z
        status=OPTIMAL
        iterations=145
        objective=1234.5678
        solve_time_sec=2.345
        [END] 2023-02-04T12:00:02Z

    /home/user/incident_triage/input/solver_b.log
        [INFO] Solver: OptiMax v2.0
        [START] 2023-02-04T12:05:00Z
        status=INFEASIBLE
        iterations=87
        objective=nan
        solve_time_sec=1.230
        [END] 2023-02-04T12:05:02Z

    /home/user/incident_triage/input/solver_c.log
        [INFO] Solver: OptiPlus v1.4
        [START] 2023-02-04T12:10:00Z
        status=TIME_LIMIT
        iterations=1000
        objective=99999.999
        solve_time_sec=60.000
        [END] 2023-02-04T12:11:00Z

    Expected state **after** the agent finishes:

    Newly created directory:
/home/user/incident_triage/output/

    /home/user/incident_triage/output/summary.csv
        solver_file,status,iterations,objective,solve_time_sec
        solver_a.log,OPTIMAL,145,1234.5678,2.345
        solver_b.log,INFEASIBLE,87,nan,1.230
        solver_c.log,TIME_LIMIT,1000,99999.999,60.000
        ⏎  (final newline)

    /home/user/incident_triage/output/alerts.txt
        solver_b.log
        solver_c.log
        ⏎  (final newline)

    No other files under /home/user/incident_triage/output/ should exist.
